ui.quickLinksDescription: Move into adjacent business modules without losing the current shell context.
ui.overviewDescription: This summary frames the module inside the product backbone.
ui.guardrailsDescription: These principles need to stay stable across the next UI sprints.
ui.deliveryDescription: New UI work should prepare later expansion without destabilizing the current workspaces.

guardrails.tenantIsolation: Tenant isolation, local visibility, and role logic must not be weakened by new screens.
guardrails.roleScope: Portals, internal administration, and partner access must respect the same authorization edges.
guardrails.documents: Documents, exports, and generated outputs must remain linkable and auditable.

deliveryPoints.ownership: New modules plug in through route metadata and a central registry pattern.
deliveryPoints.i18n: New labels, descriptions, and headers are always added in both DE and EN.
deliveryPoints.outputs: Embedded workspaces stay in place until later sprints replace them with fully native Vben-first screens.

administration: Administration keeps tenant, core, and platform configuration inside clear system boundaries.
This module is the entry point for onboarding, scope control, and product-wide baseline configuration.
Changes must support later expansion without reworking tenant isolation or the role model.
platform: Platform services encapsulate shared cross-cutting capabilities such as notices, document linkage, and integration boundaries.
Direct provider side effects do not belong in the same transaction as domain writes.
The UI needs to feel productized, without demo residue or generic sample copy.
masterData: Master-data modules organize list, detail, and release flows with stable tenant and role semantics.
Commercial and operational data stay grouped cleanly and remain referencable for downstream processes.
Portal releases, document linkage, and status changes must not introduce hidden side effects.
workforce: Workforce and recruiting modules separate private data, operational readiness, and administrative control.
Import, document, and lifecycle flows must stay consistent with later mobile and portal tracks.
Restricted roles only see the slices that their process actually needs.
partner: Partner data remains in its own truth while planning and finance consume released references only.
Scope, workforce, and commercial settings need to stay traceable and documented.
Portal capabilities build on the same visibility rules as the internal admin UI.
operations: Operational modules connect customer demand, order context, and released execution control in one chain.
Planning may read other contexts but must not hide cross-context writes behind the UI.
The interface should present filters, results, and detail work as one coherent workspace.
planning: Shift and staffing views focus on dispatching, qualifications, and release state instead of raw master data.
Planning outputs are the upstream truth for field execution, actuals, and downstream finance processes.
Calendar, coverage, and list patterns are grouped consistently through the shell instead of scattered page by page.
finance: Finance modules always work from the finance.actual_record bridge between planning, evidence, and outputs.
Raw evidence remains intact; corrections are derived in a controlled way instead of overwritten.
Exports, invoices, and checks must stay traceable as documented system outputs.
reporting: Reporting starts as a reproducible view over the transactional backbone, not as a second write-side.
Dashboards and deliveries must respect the same role and visibility rules as the operational product.
KPIs, filters, and exports are grouped into a coherent workspace instead of being treated like a demo dashboard.

dashboard.title: SicherPlan re-bootstrapped on top of Vben
dashboard.description: Navigation is now grouped by business capability, the shell follows Vben patterns, and the existing workspaces are embedded through a consistent module system.
dashboard.actionDescription: Direct entry into the areas that carry the next UI and domain sprints.
dashboard.moduleDescription: These areas form the backbone for administration, planning, and reporting.
dashboard.guardrailsDescription: These rules frame future UI and domain expansion.
dashboard.readinessDescription: The new structure makes later Codex tasks narrower, cleaner, and easier to extend.

stats.scopeHint: Tenant isolation remains the primary organizing key.
stats.visibilityHint: Admin, portal, and partner access stay readable through one role model.
stats.evidenceHint: From master data to reporting, the process chain stays traceable.

moduleMissing.title: Missing module configuration
moduleMissing.description: No SicherPlan workspace has been registered for this route yet.

core.description: Tenant setup, onboarding, and the core system are grouped into one clean administration entry point.
core.workspace: The existing core-system workspace stays active but now sits inside a consistent module page.

platformServices.description: Platform services gather notices, document linkage, and other shared services under one consistent admin frame.
platformServices.workspace: The platform workspace remains available and now lives under the same page architecture as the domain modules.

customers.description: Customer administration, commercial settings, and release visibility are clearly placed as one customer module.
customers.workspace: The customer workspace stays operational and is now embedded in the same intro, stats, and governance structure.

employees.description: Employee administration separates private HR data, operational fields, and import flows inside a workforce structure.
employees.workspace: The existing employee workspace stays intact and has been aligned to the new page grid.

workforceCatalogs.description: Function and qualification types remain HR-owned tenant master data and now live in their own workforce workspace.
workforceCatalogs.workspace: Catalog administration is separated from the employee file while staying inside the same HR domain context.

recruiting.description: Recruiting is framed as the workforce intake module for applications, review, and document handling.
recruiting.workspace: The current recruiting workspace remains linked and now follows the same page language as the other modules.

subcontractors.description: Subcontractor administration is positioned inside the workforce and portal structure as the partner-control module.
subcontractors.workspace: The partner workspace stays functional and is now consistently framed by the new shell.

planning.description: Operations acts as the entry point for execution and planning control with clean boundaries to HR and partner master data.
planning.workspace: The operational planning workspace remains directly usable and now sits under a stable module page.

planningOrders.description: Orders and planning are grouped as a dedicated workspace between customer context and execution control.
planningOrders.workspace: The existing orders workspace stays reachable and is carried by the new module shell.

planningShifts.description: Shift planning is framed as a clear planning module focused on templates, deployments, and release status.
planningShifts.workspace: The shift-planning workspace stays embedded instead of reading like a loose page dropped into the shell.

planningStaffing.description: Staffing and coverage read availability and qualification data without mutating foreign master records.
planningStaffing.workspace: The coverage workspace stays intact and now follows the same page architecture as administration and finance.

financeActuals.description: Actual approvals connect planning, evidence, and the finance.actual_record bridge as a controlled finance stage.
financeActuals.workspace: The current actuals workspace remains usable and is now embedded in the new shell pattern.

financePayroll.description: Payroll and export build on approved actuals and stay clearly separated from planning truth as a finance-owned flow.
financePayroll.workspace: The current payroll workspace remains in place and is now framed by the new module page.

financeBilling.description: Billing and invoice output stay organized as a finance-owned workspace with documented traceability.
financeBilling.workspace: The billing workspace remains operational and is now embedded in a Vben-aligned page composition.

financeSubcontractorChecks.description: Subcontractor invoice checks are positioned as a dedicated finance workspace between partner data, actuals, and settlement.
financeSubcontractorChecks.workspace: The existing review workspace stays reachable and now shares the same structured module shell as the other finance flows.

reporting.description: Reporting is detached from the demo dashboard and positioned as a SicherPlan module for reproducible, role-scoped analytics.
reporting.workspace: The reporting workspace remains active and is now embedded through the same page architecture as the operational modules.

health.description: Check the platform live, ready, and version signals together with the primary support diagnostics.
health.actionsDescription: This query reads the current health signals directly from the backend.
health.statusDescription: These signals support deployments, support work, and operational release checks.
health.loadFailed: Health status could not be loaded.

dashboardView.title: Daily control for SicherPlan
dashboardView.description: The dashboard condenses live tenant, planning, partner, and platform signals into one compact workspace and links directly into the production modules.

tenantUsers.description: Platform administration creates and manages tenant-admin login accounts per tenant with explicit tenant scope and auditable security actions.
tenantUsers.workspace: This page combines tenant selection, user listing, lifecycle controls, and password actions inside one native IAM workspace.
tenantUsers.actionsDescription: Create new tenant admins or refresh the current list for the selected tenant.
tenantUsers.filtersDescription: Select the target tenant first. The list only shows tenant_admin accounts for that scope.
tenantUsers.tableDescription: All listed accounts carry the tenant_admin role with tenant-wide scope.
tenantUsers.scopeDescription: Only platform administration creates the first tenant admin and manages security-sensitive account states.
tenantUsers.workspaceHint: The flow stays intentionally narrow: one account row, one tenant_admin assignment, one tenant scope.
tenantUsers.workspaceBody: New tenant admins receive exactly one tenant_admin assignment in the selected tenant. Lifecycle changes and password actions run through the IAM admin service and are auditable.

filter.searchPlaceholder: Search tenant by code or name

currentScope.title: Current IAM scope
currentScope.body: This page only manages tenant_admin accounts. A web UI for role catalog management, permission assignment management, or audit review is not present in the current implementation.
currentScope.infoTitle: Documentation is ahead of the current web surface
currentScope.infoBody: The repository already contains role, permission, and audit building blocks at the model, seed, and service level. There is currently no already-implemented but hidden web page for role management, permission-scope administration, or audit-event review that only needed exposure here.

empty.title: No tenant admin exists yet
empty.body: Create the first tenant-scoped admin account for the selected tenant so local administration can start immediately.
empty.table: No tenant_admin account exists for the selected tenant yet.

points: Users are always created tenant-scoped with exactly one tenant_admin assignment.
Username and email follow the existing uniqueness rules inside the tenant.
Password resets revoke active sessions and remain visible in the audit trail.
